name: NINPOU: CHAKRA NO ITO (NINJA ART: CHAKRA THREADS)

description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu

Rank: 2 (Spell Level 1)

Chakra Cost: *

Learn DC: 14 | Successes: 1

Perform: 2 | Perform DC: 13

Components: C

You create invisible Chakra threads which can be used to manipulate objects with varying degrees of control. Creatures that can See (Through) Chakra can make a Spot check (DC 10) to see the threads. Using such threads requires you have both hands free. To attach your threads to an object, you must hit it with a ranged touch attack. For each range increment beyond the first a controlled object is distanced from you, you take a -2 penalty to all checks and rolls made through or with it.

Each time a controlled object is used to deliver damage, you must make an Intelligence check (DC 20) to avoid losing your grip on the object until your next turn. Chakra threads come in various classes, each with its own Chakra cost and statistics:

| Class | Range Increment | HP | Trip | Spot DC | Objects | Cost |
|---------|-------------------|------|--------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| D | 10 ft | 7 | 10 ft | 10 | 2 | 1 |
| C | 20 ft | 11 | 15 ft | 15 | 3 | 2 |
| B | 30 ft | 15 | 20 ft | 20 | 4 | 3 |
| A | 50 ft | 20 | 30 ft | 25 | 5 | 4 |

A chakra thread can reach up to five range increments and can be used to deliver a Trip or Disarm at- tempt at range (as indicated). You do not risk getting tripped or disarmed if you fail such an attempt.

More powerful threads become increasingly difficult to spot (as shown) and can be used control multiple objects, though each object is controlled individually and a puppeteer is still limited by its (Advanced) Puppetry ability in how many puppets it can control.

Chakra threads have a Defense of 10 plus your Dexterity modifier, and can only be damaged by slashing attacks that can bypass Chakra-based damage reduction. A thread that is destroyed will disconnect you from whatever object that thread controlled. Severed threads can be reformed as a swift action while this technique is still active, but you will have to pay the cost again.

Although they can manipulate objects at range, you still use your Strength score to determine the total weight of items you can carry, lift or move.

The basic use of this technique creates D-class threads. Mastery allows you to create higher class threads.

- D-Class: These threads manipulate object as though they were a rope. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Trip or Disarm attempts made with them, and you suffer a -4 penalty to attacks rolls and skill checks requiring precision made with or through the objects.
- C-Class: These threads manipulate object as though using a weak telekinetic force, allowing you to nudge it in any direction, and you suffer a -2 penalty to attacks rolls and skill checks requiring precision made with or through the objects.
- B-Class: These threads manipulate objects as though they were a telekinetic force, and increase your effective Strength score by 2 when lifting or moving objects.
- A-Class: These threads can manipulate objects as though using your own hands and increase your effective Strength score by 5 when lifting or moving objects.

Mastery: Each step grants a +1 bonus to the touch attack made to attach threads to an inanimate object.

The first step requires five times as long to learn, and allows you to create C-Class threads.

The second step allows you to perform this technique as a move action up to three times per encounter.

The third step requires ten times as long to learn, and allows you to create B-class threads.

The fourth step allows you to perform this technique as a swift action up to three times per encounter.

The fifth step takes twenty times as long to learn, and allows you to create A-Class threads.
